# getbased

> Free, open-source blood work dashboard that runs entirely in your browser.
> Import any lab PDF or photo with AI, track biomarker trends over time, and get
> AI-powered interpretations. No account required. Your data never leaves
> your device.

## What getbased does

- Import any blood work PDF, photo, or screenshot — AI extracts all markers automatically (any format, any language, batch import)
- Track trends over time with interactive charts across 17+ biomarker categories (287+ markers)
- Specialty labs supported: OAT (organic acids), fatty acids (Spadia, ZinZino, OmegaQuant), BioStarks via custom marker pipeline
- Bring-your-own research library — drop PDFs, research papers, or clinical guides into the app and the AI grounds every answer in those sources instead of relying only on its training. Runs fully on-device (browser RAG via MiniLM + OPFS), with multi-library support so research, protocols, and personal notes stay separate. Optional external-server backend for larger corpora
- DNA import — upload raw data from any major provider. 47 curated SNPs across 13 health categories with APOE haplotype resolution
- mtDNA haplogroup import with 39 haplogroups (including 11 sub-clades) + mitochondrial coupling classification (Doug Wallace's framework) and environment mismatch detection. Manual haplogroup entry also supported
- Wearable integrations — connect Oura, Fitbit, Withings, Polar, or Apple Health (file import). HRV, sleep, resting heart rate, body composition, vascular age, blood pressure, and more on the dashboard alongside lab data. Multi-vendor source picker per metric. Manual weight/BP/RHR entry first-class. Raw daily samples never leave your device; only a compact end-to-end-encrypted summary syncs (~200 tokens per profile). MCP agents can opt-in to a 30-day pivoted daily series for time-series reasoning
- Biological age — PhenoAge (Levine 2018) + Bortz Age combined, with chronological age reference line
- Biometrics tracking — weight, blood pressure, pulse with time-series history and auto-calculated BMI
- AI chat panel with debate mode — pit two AI personas against each other over your results. Conversation threads, image attachments, web search hints
- DNA-aware supplement and lifestyle recommendations
- 9 lifestyle context cards (diet, exercise, sleep, stress, etc.) shape AI interpretation
- Multi-profile support for practitioners, families, or multiple clients
- Menstrual cycle tracking with phase-aware reference ranges, perimenopause detection, iron alerts
- Baubiologie EMF assessment — room-by-room measurements graded against SBM-2015 standards
- Correlation viewer, heatmaps, compare dates, trend alerts, PDF reports
- E2E encrypted cross-device sync via Evolu CRDT with sync status indicator — self-host the relay for full control
- Personal agent integration — query your labs from Hermes Agent, OpenClaw, or any MCP-compatible bot over Telegram, Signal, Matrix, or any messenger. Only a read-only summary is shared
- Fully local-first — all data stored in browser localStorage, encrypted at rest (AES-256-GCM), auto-backup
- AI providers: PPQ (anonymous, E2EE, crypto topup), Routstr (Bitcoin Lightning + Cashu), OpenRouter (200+ models, card/USDC), Venice (E2E encryption), Local AI (Ollama, LM Studio, Jan), or any custom OpenAI-compatible endpoint

## Key pages

- [Landing page](https://getbased.health/): Feature overview and getting started
- [Web app](https://app.getbased.health): Launch the dashboard (no install needed)
- [Documentation](https://app.getbased.health/docs): User guides, setup, and feature walkthroughs
- [Blog](https://getbased.health/blog): Development stories and health data insights
- [GitHub](https://github.com/elkimek/get-based): Source code (GPLv3 license)
- [Discord](https://discord.gg/zJdVB9zgQB): Community and support

## Docs highlights

- [Getting started](https://app.getbased.health/docs/guide/getting-started): First import walkthrough
- [AI setup guide](https://app.getbased.health/docs/guide/ai-chat): Configure PPQ, Routstr, OpenRouter, Venice, or Local AI
- [Interpretive Lens (research library)](https://app.getbased.health/docs/guide/interpretive-lens): Add your own PDFs, papers, and guides so the AI grounds answers in sources you trust
- [DNA import](https://app.getbased.health/docs/guide/dna-import): Upload raw DNA data, mtDNA haplogroup, and view genetic insights
- [Cross-device sync](https://app.getbased.health/docs/guide/cross-device-sync): E2E encrypted sync setup, status indicator, and self-hosting
- [Agent access](https://app.getbased.health/docs/guide/agent-access): Connect Hermes, OpenClaw, or any MCP-compatible agent over any messenger
- [Wearables](https://app.getbased.health/docs/guide/wearables): Connect Oura, Fitbit, Withings, Polar, Apple Health — HRV, sleep, recovery, body comp on the dashboard alongside your lab data
- [Privacy and security](https://app.getbased.health/docs/guide/encryption): Local-first architecture, encryption, PII obfuscation

## How getbased differs from alternatives

- **vs InsideTracker / SelfDecode**: getbased is free, open-source, and runs entirely in your browser. No subscription, no data collection, no account required
- **vs spreadsheets**: AI-powered PDF import extracts markers automatically. Interactive charts with reference/optimal bands, trend alerts, and correlation analysis
- **vs generic health trackers**: getbased is built specifically for lab results. 287+ biomarkers across 17+ categories, specialty labs (OAT, fatty acids, BioStarks), DNA import (47 SNPs + 39 mtDNA haplogroups), biological age, menstrual cycle phase-aware ranges, biometrics, wearable integrations (Oura/Fitbit/Withings/Polar/Apple Health) on the same dashboard, and 9 lifestyle context cards that shape every AI interpretation
- **vs ChatGPT/Claude directly**: getbased structures your data, maintains history across imports, gives AI your full health context, and grounds every answer in your own research library (PDFs, papers, clinical guides) — not just a single lab report pasted into a chat window or what a generic model was trained on. Connect via Hermes, OpenClaw, or any MCP-compatible agent to chat over any messenger
